prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "猫牛式 (牛相)" (cat-cow stretch (cow phase)) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person in a quadruped position performing the COW phase of the cat-cow stretch: hands flat on the ground directly under shoulders (arms straight, fingers spread), knees on the ground directly under hips. The back is gently ARCHED downward (lumbar extension) — belly drops toward the floor, chest lifts up and forward, the head tilts up to look slightly forward and up. This is the OPEN/COW phase. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: erector spinae and rectus abdominis (eccentric stretch through spinal flexion-extension cycle). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: thoracic spine mobilizers and pelvic floor muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "竖脊肌" pointing to the erector spinae; "腹直肌" pointing to the rectus abdominis (under stretch in cow phase); "胸椎活动肌" pointing to the thoracic spine mobilizers. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
